author: Austin Crawford
title: Breakfast with the Chief
summary: “Meet and eat” with Chief Moynihan and hear the plans for summer 2022. Saturday, May 21, 2022, from 8:30 to 10:30 a.m. at the Block
content:
“Meet and eat” with Chief Moynihan and hear the plans for summer 2022. Saturday, May 21, 2022, from 8:30 to 10:30 a.m. at the Block
Island Maritime Institute. Adults $10 each, and children under 12 are free. Tickets available at the Block Island Chamber of Commerce, Island Free Library, The Cracked Mug,
and Island Bound Bookstore. Sponsored by BIRA and Kimberly’s. Any excess profits will go to the Block Island Rescue Squad.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Arbor Day Celebration - Block Island School
summary: The Block Island Gardeners will celebrate Arbor Day at the Block Island School with the presentation of red maple saplings to students in grades K to 7. Parents and friends are invited to attend. The red maple is native to the continental USA and Canada, and its north-south range (from Newfoundland to southern Florida) is the greatest of any tree species in the eastern forests. Red maple was adopted as the state tree of Rhode Island in 1964.
content: The Block Island Gardeners will celebrate Arbor Day at the Block Island School with the presentation of red maple saplings to students in grades K to 7. Parents and friends are invited to attend. The red maple is native to the continental USA and Canada, and its north-south range (from Newfoundland to southern Florida) is the greatest of any tree species in the eastern forests. Red maple was adopted as the state tree of Rhode Island in 1964.

ter-Christmas bird walk. Contact Kim with questions at kim.gaffett@tnc.org.
address: Sachem Pond New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Zoom on Nature with Coffee and Kim Resumes

author: Will Gasner
title: 35th Annual Hal Madison Race
content:
The Block Island Club will host the 35th Annual Hal Madison Race on Saturday, August 11.

There will be a skippers' meeting at the Block Island Club on Corn Neck Road
at 10:00 AM. The first gun will be at 12:00 noon, with the starting line off the west
side of the island near the entrance to the Great Salt Pond. There will be an
around-the-buoys course determined on race day by the race committee. Awards
will be presented at the club's August members' party in the evening following the

race.

PHRF handicapping will be used. It is expected that there will be a spinnaker
and non-spinnaker (cruising) class. Dinghies are also encouraged to race; if more
than one dinghy registers, we will have a dinghy class. Entry fees are $40 ($30 for
dinghies) in advance and $50 ($35 for dinghies) on the day of race.
Don’t own a boat?? No problem! Colgate 26 one-design sloop keelboats are

available for charter at the Block Island Club.
To register online, please visit:

http://www.regattanetwork.com/event/17430
Hal Madison was a founding member and former president of the
Block Island Club; Hal was an early proponent of its junior sailing program.
There will be an awards presentation with food and drinks

following the race at 6 PM at the Block Island Club; all sailors are welcome.
Any questions should be directed to the Block Island Club at 401-466-5939.

For additional info, please visit: www.blockislandclub.org

 

Register online here: http://www.regattanetwork.com/event/17430

author: Austin Crawford
title: James Stover Exploration Series
summary: Cormorant Cove Walk: Explore the shoreline from Cormorant Cove to Charlestown Beach and the channel entrance into New Harbor. The route will depend on which way the wind is blowing. Sightings will likely include eiders, shellfish, and seals. Meet at Coast Guard Station, Sat., Feb. 26 at 10 a.m. To sign up, email kim.gaffett@tnc.org.
